Title: A plant factory for moth pheromone production

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables higher transient transgene expression in N. benthamiana, supporting production of moth pheromone pathway metabolites
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Attract male moths; used for pest monitoring and mating disruption
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-infiltration in Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression with metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. P19 was co-introduced to inhibit host transgene silencing. 2. This extended transgene expression and increased expression level for the introduced biosynthetic genes. 3. It functioned as an enabling component rather than a direct biosynthetic enzyme.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a metabolism gene per se, but part of the experimental system repeatedly used in the Results and field-production setup.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enabling module for transient multigene metabolic engineering in plants

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes the thioester bond of myristoyl-ACP to release myristic acid, increasing export from plastid and availability for 14-carbon pheromone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Attract male moths; used for pest monitoring and disruption
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS lipid profiling; combinatorial pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases myristic acid precursor pool, enabling production of 14-carbon pheromone components
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpFATB2 expression increased myristic acid in leaves from <2 μg g−1 to generally >200 μg g−1 fresh tissue. 2. It created a cytosolic 14:CoA pool required for downstream desaturation and reduction. 3. It was essential for efficient production of 14-carbon pheromone components in plants.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter endogenous fatty acid flux and lipid composition
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important precursor-supply module for engineering plant production of 14-carbon fatty acid-derived compounds
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 16-carbon pheromones were produced from preexisting cytosolic 16:CoA and did not require this enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a double bond at the Δ11 position of palmitoyl-CoA to form (Z)-11-hexadecenoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Attract male moths; species-specific pheromone component
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS metabolite profiling; pathway combination for field-production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables accumulation of Z11-16 acyl/alcohol/acetate products; selected for 16-carbon pheromone production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-determining desaturation step for 16-carbon product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtrD11 showed high substrate specificity toward 16:CoA. 2. It produced Z11-16:acyl with 49.5 ± 2.8% conversion relative to precursor. 3. It was selected for large-scale production of 16-carbon pheromone components.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful module for engineering Z11-16-derived pheromones in plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Natural ER localization inferred from desaturase membrane topology and pathway context.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a double bond at the Δ11 position of myristoyl-CoA to form both (E)-11- and (Z)-11-tetradecenoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Attract male moths; species-specific pheromone components
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS profiling; combinatorial pathway reconstruction; field-production setup
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates the E/Z11-14 unsaturated precursor mixture needed for 14-carbon moth pheromone components
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-determining desaturation step for 14-carbon products
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AveD11 specifically interacted with 14:CoA. 2. It produced both E11-14:acyl and Z11-14:acyl at 9.3 ± 0.5% and 33.0 ± 1.9% conversion, respectively. 3. It was selected for larger scale production despite generating a nonoptimal E/Z ratio for some target moths.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: E/Z product ratio was not optimal for maximal attraction of target species
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core module for production of 14-carbon unsaturated pheromone blends; engineering of product ratio is desirable
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene was synthesized and codon-optimized for N. benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces saturated and unsaturated fatty acyl-CoAs to the corresponding primary fatty alcohols
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Direct pheromone components or precursors to acetate pheromones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS profiling; comparison with other FARs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts unsaturated acyl precursors into fatty alcohol pheromone components, with better performance than YroFAR and OnuFAR variants
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established, but reduction was less efficient than desaturation and represented a bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HarFAR showed higher conversion rates than YroFAR in plants. 2. Reduction was less efficient than desaturation, indicating this step as a performance bottleneck. 3. HarFAR was chosen for further optimization and large-scale production.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower conversion efficiency than desaturation limits overall pathway yield
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key reductase module for plant production of moth pheromone alcohols; should be optimized or replaced for higher flux
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: C-terminal KKYR modification improved performance; the modified form was selected for optimization experiments.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Fatty acyl reductase with engineered C-terminal ER retention signal KKYR
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces fatty acyl-CoAs to corresponding primary alcohols; ER retention signal increases effective activity in plant cells
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Direct pheromone components or precursors for acetate pheromones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein engineering of FAR; transient expression; GC-MS quantification; statistical comparison with wild type HarFAR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves fatty alcohol production over wild-type HarFAR and was selected as the most suitable reductase candidate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Improved version of a bottleneck reduction step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Addition of the C-terminal KKYR ER retention signal significantly improved alcohol production for multiple substrates. 2. Conversion increased to 14.5% for 14:OH, 28.2% for E11-14:OH, 19.5% for Z11-14:OH, and 5.2% for Z11-16:OH. 3. This was the best-performing FAR variant tested and was selected for larger scale production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates that subcellular engineering can improve pathway flux; useful for metabolic engineering of fatty alcohol products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Engineered variant, not a native gene sequence.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetate group to long-chain primary alcohols to form acetate pheromone components
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Active moth sex pheromone components for attracting males
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts pheromone alcohols into acetate pheromone components, but with relatively low efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal conversion step with suboptimal efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EaDAcT acetylated 14C and 16C fatty alcohols to corresponding acetates. 2. Conversion rates were ~5–18%, considered low for practical production. 3. This step was identified as needing future improvement, likely through discovery of a more suitable insect enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Incomplete acetylation leaves residual alcohols that may reduce attractiveness
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful proof-of-principle terminal acetylation module, but not ideal for final deployment without optimization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen because no moth acetyltransferase had yet been characterized.
